reasoning:
The 360-day average of ~128 protests/month was sustained by the Hostages Families Forum organizing protests across 50-80 nationwide locations every week. In October 2025, a hostage and ceasefire deal was finalized (Trump's deadline was Oct 5, Israeli cabinet approved it around Oct 10). After the deal, widespread grassroots hostage protests largely ended, with the Families Forum actively winding down and closing by Dec 31. To exceed the 128 mark in a 30-day period, an average of ~32 separate protest events per week is required. 
1. The 30 days before Nov 2 (Oct 3 - Nov 2) include 1 week of peak pre-deal protests but 3 weeks of post-deal lull, making it highly probable the total falls short of the 128 threshold (which requires 4 active weekends).
2. The 30 days before Nov 25 (Oct 26 - Nov 25) sit firmly in the post-deal lull, with very few hostage protests and before anti-government protests fully resumed, likely resulting in a very low event count.
3. The 30 days before Jan 24 (Dec 25 - Jan 24) cover the resurgence of weekly anti-government protests following Netanyahu's December 2025 pardon request. However, purely political rallies tend to concentrate thousands of people into a few major cities rather than dozens of local junctions, making it doubtful they will exceed the wartime geographical spread measured by ACLED. 
Uncertainty increases for more distant dates as new issues (like the Lebanon anti-war movement) could shift dynamics, naturally regressing predictions closer to 0.5.
